audience: Non-technical users, customers, operators, and demo viewers

workflows.label: Primary request
workflows.description: Shows how a user request moves from input to a visible product result.

stages.label: User submits input
stages.whatHappens: The UI captures the user's intent and creates a traceable run.
stages.input: Redacted concept, item, or task label
stages.output: Run id and safe request summary
stages.risk: Invalid input, empty input, or duplicate request

stages.label: Server receives request
stages.whatHappens: The route validates parameters and starts the workflow.

stages.label: Core processing
stages.whatHappens: The product performs the core business or AI operation.
stages.input: Redacted task category and safe options
stages.risk: Timeout, upstream error, or partial response

stages.label: Render result
stages.whatHappens: The UI merges returned data and renders the final result.
stages.risk: Partial render or degraded visual layer

events.name: primary-request.processed
events.safeSummary: The core operation returned a structured result.

workflows.label: Background import
workflows.description: Shows how a batch or file import moves through queued processing.

stages.label: Import starts
stages.whatHappens: The user selects source material and starts an import run.
stages.input: Redacted source label and item count

stages.label: Job enters queue
stages.whatHappens: The backend records the import and schedules background work.

stages.label: Items are processed
stages.whatHappens: The worker parses, validates, and stores importable items.
stages.risk: Partial import, malformed rows, or upstream timeout

stages.label: Import completes
stages.whatHappens: The product makes the imported items available to the user.

events.name: background-import.processed
events.safeSummary: Items were processed with safe counts.

redactionRules: Never show API keys, auth tokens, secrets, or environment values.
Never show raw system prompts or complete model prompts.
Never show full request or response bodies.
Never show personal data unless the user explicitly provided consent and the product policy allows it.
Show only workflow ids, run ids, stage names, counts, durations, provider labels, retry state, cache state, and redacted labels.
